translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Since many have undertaken to set in order a narrative concerning those matters which have been fulfilled among us,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: even as those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and servants of the word delivered them to us,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: it seemed good to me also, having traced the course of all things accurately from the first, to write to you in order, most excellent Theophilus;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that you might know the certainty concerning the things in which you were instructed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the priestly division of Abijah. He had a wife of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were both righteous before God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they both were well advanced in years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now while he executed the priest’s office before God in the order of his division,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: according to the custom of the priest’s office, his lot was to enter into the temple of the Lord and burn incense.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The whole multitude of the people were praying outside at the hour of incense.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: An angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing on the right side of the altar of incense.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zacharias was troubled when he saw him, and fear fell upon him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the angel said to him, “Don’t be afraid, Zacharias, because your request has been heard, and your wife, Elizabeth, will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You will have joy and gladness; and many will rejoice at his birth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord, and he will drink no wine nor strong drink. He will be filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother’s womb.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord, their God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will go before him in the spirit and power of Elijah, ‘to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children,’ and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just; to prepare a people prepared for the Lord.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zacharias said to the angel, “How can I be sure of this? For I am an old man, and my wife is well advanced in years.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The angel answered him, “I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God. I was sent to speak to you, and to bring you this good news.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold,  you will be silent and not able to speak, until the day that these things will happen, because you didn’t believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their proper time.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The people were waiting for Zacharias, and they marveled that he delayed in the temple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he came out, he could not speak to them, and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple. He continued making signs to them, and remained mute.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the days of his service were fulfilled, he departed to his house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After these days Elizabeth, his wife, conceived, and she hid herself five months, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Thus has the Lord done to me in the days in which he looked at me, to take away my reproach among men.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now in the sixth month, the angel Gabriel was sent from God to a city of Galilee, named Nazareth,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to a virgin pledged to be married to a man whose name was Joseph, of David’s house. The virgin’s name was Mary.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Having come in, the angel said to her, “Rejoice, you highly favored one! The Lord is with you. Blessed are you among women!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when she saw him, she was greatly troubled at the saying, and considered what kind of salutation this might be.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The angel said to her, “Don’t be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, you will conceive in your womb, and give birth to a son, and will call his name ‘Jesus.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Most High. The Lord God will give him the throne of his father, David,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he will reign over the house of Jacob forever. There will be no end to his Kingdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mary said to the angel, “How can this be, seeing I am a virgin?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The angel answered her, “The Holy Spirit will come on you, and the power of the Most High will overshadow you. Therefore also the holy one who is born from you will be called the Son of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, Elizabeth, your relative, also has conceived a son in her old age; and this is the sixth month with her who was called barren.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For nothing spoken by God is impossible.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mary said, “Behold, the servant of the Lord; let it be done to me according to your word.” The angel departed from her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mary arose in those days and went into the hill country with haste, into a city of Judah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and entered into the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Elizabeth heard Mary’s greeting, the baby leaped in her womb, and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She called out with a loud voice, and said, “Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why am I so favored, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For behold, when the voice of your greeting came into my ears, the baby leaped in my womb for joy!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is she who believed, for there will be a fulfillment of the things which have been spoken to her from the Lord!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mary said, “My soul magnifies the Lord.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for he has looked at the humble state of his servant. For behold, from now on, all generations will call me blessed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he who is mighty has done great things for me. Holy is his name.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His mercy is for generations of generations on those who fear him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has shown strength with his arm. He has scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has put down princes from their thrones. And has exalted the lowly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has filled the hungry with good things. He has sent the rich away empty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He has given help to Israel, his servant, that he might remember mercy,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he spoke to our fathers, to Abraham and his offspring  forever.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Mary stayed with her about three months, and then returned to her house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the time that Elizabeth should give birth was fulfilled, and she gave birth to a son.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her neighbors and her relatives heard that the Lord had magnified his mercy towards her, and they rejoiced with her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the eighth day, they came to circumcise the child; and they would have called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His mother answered, “Not so; but he will be called John.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to her, “There is no one among your relatives who is called by this name.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They made signs to his father, what he would have him called.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He asked for a writing tablet, and wrote, “His name is John.” They all marveled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His mouth was opened immediately, and his tongue freed, and he spoke, blessing God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fear came on all who lived around them, and all these sayings were talked about throughout all the hill country of Judea.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All who heard them laid them up in their heart, saying, “What then will this child be?” The hand of the Lord was with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His father, Zacharias, was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, for he has visited and redeemed his people;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (as he spoke by the mouth of his holy prophets who have been from of old),

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: salvation from our enemies, and from the hand of all who hate us;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to show mercy towards our fathers, to remember his holy covenant,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the oath which he swore to Abraham, our father,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to grant to us that we, being delivered out of the hand of our enemies, should serve him without fear,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In holiness and righteousness before him all the days of our life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: And you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High, for you will go before the face of the Lord to prepare his ways,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to give knowledge of salvation to his people by the remission of their sins,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: because of the tender mercy of our God, whereby the dawn from on high will visit us,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to shine on those who sit in darkness and the shadow of death; to guide our feet into the way of peace.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The child was growing, and becoming strong in spirit, and was in the desert until the day of his public appearance to Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now in those days, a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be enrolled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This was the first enrollment made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All went to enroll themselves, everyone to his own city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judea, to David’s city, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and family of David;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to enroll himself with Mary, who was pledged to be married to him as wife, being pregnant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While they were there, the day had come for her to give birth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him in bands of cloth, and laid him in a feeding trough, because there was no room for them in the inn.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were shepherds in the same country staying in the field, and keeping watch by night over their flock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, an angel of the Lord stood by them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were terrified.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The angel said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for behold, I bring you good news of great joy which will be to all the people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For there is born to you today, in David’s city, a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is the sign to you: you will find a baby wrapped in strips of cloth, lying in a feeding trough.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Suddenly, there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly army praising God, and saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Glory to God in the highest, on earth peace, good will toward men.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the angels went away from them into the sky, the shepherds said to one another, “Let’s go to Bethlehem, now, and see this thing that has happened, which the Lord has made known to us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came with haste, and found both Mary and Joseph, and the baby was lying in the feeding trough.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they saw it, they publicized widely the saying which was spoken to them about this child.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All who heard it wondered at the things which were spoken to them by the shepherds.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Mary kept all these sayings, pondering them in her heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, just as it was told them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When eight days were fulfilled for the circumcision of the child, his name was called Jesus, which was given by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the days of their purification according to the law of Moses were fulfilled, they brought him up to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every male who opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord”),

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and to offer a sacrifice according to that which is said in the law of the Lord, “A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon. This man was righteous and devout, looking for the consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he should not see death before he had seen the Lord’s Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came in the Spirit into the temple. When the parents brought in the child, Jesus, that they might do concerning him according to the custom of the law,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then he received him into his arms, and blessed God, and said,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now you are releasing your servant, Master, according to your word, in peace;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for my eyes have seen your salvation,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: which you have prepared before the face of all peoples;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: a light for revelation to the nations, and the glory of your people Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Joseph and his mother were marveling at the things which were spoken concerning him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary, his mother, “Behold, this child is set for the falling and the rising of many in Israel, and for a sign which is spoken against.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, a sword will pierce through your own soul, that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was one Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher (she was of a great age, having lived with a husband seven years from her virginity,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and she had been a widow for about eighty-four years), who didn’t depart from the temple, worshiping with fastings and petitions night and day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Coming up at that very hour, she gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke of him to all those who were looking for redemption in Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had accomplished all things that were according to the law of the Lord, they returned into Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The child was growing, and was becoming strong in spirit, being filled with wisdom, and the grace of God was upon him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His parents went every year to Jerusalem at the feast of the Passover.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to the custom of the feast,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and when they had fulfilled the days, as they were returning, the boy Jesus stayed behind in Jerusalem. Joseph and his mother didn’t know it,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but supposing him to be in the company, they went a day’s journey, and they looked for him among their relatives and acquaintances.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they didn’t find him, they returned to Jerusalem, looking for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After three days they found him in the temple, sitting in the middle of the teachers, both listening to them, and asking them questions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All who heard him were amazed at his understanding and his answers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they saw him, they were astonished, and his mother said to him, “Son, why have you treated us this way? Behold, your father and I were anxiously looking for you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Didn’t you know that I must be in my Father’s house?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They didn’t understand the saying which he spoke to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: And he went down with them, and came to Nazareth. He was subject to them, and his mother kept all these sayings in her heart.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judea, and Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of the region of Ituraea and Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: in the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John, the son of Zacharias, in the wilderness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came into all the region around the Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for remission of sins.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make ready the way of the Lord. Make his paths straight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Every valley will be filled. Every mountain and hill will be brought low. The crooked will become straight, and the rough ways smooth.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All flesh will see God’s salvation.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said therefore to the multitudes who went out to be baptized by him, “You offspring of vipers, who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore produce fruits worthy of repentance, and don’t begin to say among yourselves, ‘We have Abraham for our father;’ for I tell you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even now the ax also lies at the root of the trees. Every tree therefore that doesn’t produce good fruit is cut down, and thrown into the fire.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The multitudes asked him, “What then must we do?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered them, “He who has two coats, let him give to him who has none. He who has food, let him do likewise.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tax collectors also came to be baptized, and they said to him, “Teacher, what must we do?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Collect no more than that which is appointed to you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Soldiers also asked him, saying, “What about us? What must we do?” He said to them, “Extort from no one by violence, neither accuse anyone wrongfully. Be content with your wages.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As the people were in expectation, and all men reasoned in their hearts concerning John, whether perhaps he was the Christ,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: John answered them all, “I indeed baptize you with water, but he comes who is mightier than I, the strap of whose sandals I am not worthy to loosen. He will baptize you in the Holy Spirit and fire,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: whose fan is in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his threshing floor, and will gather the wheat into his barn; but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then with many other exhortations he preached good news to the people,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but Herod the tetrarch,  being reproved by him for Herodias, his brother’s  wife, and for all the evil things which Herod had done,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: added this also to them all, that he shut up John in prison.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when all the people were baptized, Jesus also had been baptized, and was praying. The sky was opened,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and the Holy Spirit descended in a bodily form like a dove on him; and a voice came out of the sky, saying “You are my beloved Son. In you I am well pleased.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus himself, when he began to teach, was about thirty years old, being the son (as was supposed) of Joseph, the son of Heli,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semein, the son of Joseph, the son of Judah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Joanan, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmodam, the son of Er,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Jose, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonan, the son of Eliakim,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Melea, the son of Menan, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Amminadab, the son of Aram,  the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalaleel, the son of Cainan,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the son of Enos, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for forty days, being tempted by the devil. He ate nothing in those days. Afterward, when they were completed, he was hungry.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered him, saying, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The devil, leading him up on a high mountain, showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The devil said to him, “I will give you all this authority, and their glory, for it has been delivered to me; and I give it to whomever I want.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you therefore will worship before me, it will all be yours.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered him, “Get behind me Satan! For it is written, ‘You shall worship the Lord your God, and you shall serve him only.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He led him to Jerusalem, and set him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, “If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for it is written, ‘He will put his angels in charge of you, to guard you;’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and, ‘On their hands they will bear you up, lest perhaps you dash your foot against a stone.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answering, said to him, “It has been said, ‘You shall not tempt the Lord your God.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the devil had completed every temptation, he departed from him until another time.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit into Galilee, and news about him spread through all the surrounding area.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up. He entered, as was his custom, into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood up to read.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The book of the prophet Isaiah was handed to him. He opened the book, and found the place where it was written,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The Spirit of the Lord is on me,  because he has anointed me to preach good news to the poor.  He has sent me to heal the broken hearted,   to proclaim release to the captives,  recovering of sight to the blind,  to deliver those who are crushed,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and to proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He closed the book, gave it back to the attendant, and sat down. The eyes of all in the synagogue were fastened on him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He began to tell them, “Today, this Scripture has been fulfilled in your hearing.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All testified about him, and wondered at the gracious words which proceeded out of his mouth, and they said, “Isn’t this Joseph’s son?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Doubtless you will tell me this parable, ‘Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done at Capernaum, do also here in your hometown.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Most certainly I tell you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But truly I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the sky was shut up three years and six months, when a great famine came over all the land.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Elijah was sent to none of them, except to Zarephath, in the land of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, yet not one of them was cleansed, except Naaman, the Syrian.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were all filled with wrath in the synagogue, as they heard these things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They rose up, threw him out of the city, and led him to the brow of the hill that their city was built on, that they might throw him off the cliff.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he, passing through the middle of them, went his way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee. He was teaching them on the Sabbath day,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and they were astonished at his teaching, for his word was with authority.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the synagogue there was a man who had a spirit of an unclean demon, and he cried out with a loud voice,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, “Ah! what have we to do with you, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know you who you are: the Holy One of God!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus rebuked him, saying, “Be silent, and come out of him!”  When the demon had thrown him down in the middle of them, he came out of him, having done him no harm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amazement came on all, and they spoke together, one with another, saying, “What is this word? For with authority and power he commands the unclean spirits, and they come out!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: News about him went out into every place of the surrounding region.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He rose up from the synagogue, and entered into Simon’s house. Simon’s mother-in-law was afflicted with a great fever, and they begged him for her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He stood over her, and rebuked the fever; and it left her. Immediately she rose up and served them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the sun was setting, all those who had any sick with various diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hands on every one of them, and healed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Demons also came out of many, crying out, and saying, “You are the Christ, the Son of God!” Rebuking them, he didn’t allow them to speak, because they knew that he was the Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When it was day, he departed and went into an uninhabited place, and the multitudes looked for him, and came to him, and held on to him, so that he wouldn’t go away from them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said to them, “I must preach the good news of God’s Kingdom to the other cities also. For this reason I have been sent.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now while the multitude pressed on him and heard the word of God, he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He saw two boats standing by the lake, but the fishermen had gone out of them, and were washing their nets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He entered into one of the boats, which was Simon’s, and asked him to put out a little from the land. He sat down and taught the multitudes from the boat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into the deep, and let down your nets for a catch.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Simon answered him, “Master, we worked all night, and took nothing; but at your word I will let down the net.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had done this, they caught a great multitude of fish, and their net was breaking.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They beckoned to their partners in the other boat, that they should come and help them. They came, and filled both boats, so that they began to sink.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Simon Peter, when he saw it, fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, “Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he was amazed, and all who were with him, at the catch of fish which they had caught;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. Jesus said to Simon, “Don’t be afraid. From now on you will be catching people alive.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they had brought their boats to land, they left everything, and followed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he was in one of the cities, behold, there was a man full of leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face, and begged him, saying, “Lord, if you want to, you can make me clean.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He stretched out his hand, and touched him, saying, “I want to. Be made clean.” Immediately the leprosy left him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He commanded him to tell no one, “But go your way, and show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing according to what Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the report concerning him spread much more, and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he withdrew himself into the desert, and prayed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On one of those days, he was teaching; and there were Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting by, who had come out of every village of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem. The power of the Lord was with him to heal them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, men brought a paralyzed man on a cot, and they sought to bring him in to lay before Jesus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Not finding a way to bring him in because of the multitude, they went up to the housetop, and let him down through the tiles with his cot into the middle before Jesus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Seeing their faith, he said to him, “Man, your sins are forgiven you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, “Who is this that speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, answered them, “Why are you reasoning so in your hearts?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Which is easier to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you;’ or to say, ‘Arise and walk?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” (he said to the paralyzed man), “I tell you, arise, and take up your cot, and go to your house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately he rose up before them, and took up that which he was laying on, and departed to his house, glorifying God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Amazement took hold on all, and they glorified God. They were filled with fear, saying, “We have seen strange things today.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After these things he went out, and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax office, and said to him, “Follow me!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He left everything, and rose up and followed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Levi made a great feast for him in his house. There was a great crowd of tax collectors and others who were reclining with them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Their scribes and the Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with the tax collectors and sinners?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick do.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said to him, “Why do John’s disciples often fast and pray, likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast, while the bridegroom is with them?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them. Then they will fast in those days.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also told a parable to them. “No one puts a piece from a new garment on an old garment, or else he will tear the new, and also the piece from the new will not match the old.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No one puts new wine into old wine skins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But new wine must be put into fresh wine skins, and both are preserved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No man having drunk old wine immediately desires new, for he says, ‘The old is better.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now on the second Sabbath after the first, he was going through the grain fields. His disciples plucked the heads of grain and ate, rubbing them in their hands.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But some of the Pharisees said to them, “Why do you do that which is not lawful to do on the Sabbath day?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, answering them, said, “Haven’t you read what David did when he was hungry, he, and those who were with him;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: how he entered into God’s house, and took and ate the show bread, and gave also to those who were with him, which is not lawful to eat except for the priests alone?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “The Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It also happened on another Sabbath that he entered into the synagogue and taught. There was a man there, and his right hand was withered.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The scribes and the Pharisees watched him, to see whether he would heal on the Sabbath, that they might find an accusation against him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he knew their thoughts; and he said to the man who had the withered hand, “Rise up, and stand in the middle.” He arose and stood.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus said to them, “I will ask you something: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good, or to do harm? To save a life, or to kill?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He looked around at them all, and said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his hand was restored as sound as the other.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they were filled with rage, and talked with one another about what they might do to Jesus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In these days, he went out to the mountain to pray, and he continued all night in prayer to God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When it was day, he called his disciples, and from them he chose twelve, whom he also named apostles:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Simon, whom he also named Peter; Andrew, his brother; James; John; Philip; Bartholomew;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Matthew; Thomas; James, the son of Alphaeus; Simon, who was called the Zealot;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Judas the son of James; and Judas Iscariot, who also became a traitor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came down with them, and stood on a level place, with a crowd of his disciples, and a great number of the people from all Judea and Jerusalem, and the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear him and to be healed of their diseases;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: as well as those who were troubled by unclean spirits, and they were being healed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the multitude sought to touch him, for power came out of him and healed them all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He lifted up his eyes to his disciples, and said,  “Blessed are you who are poor,  God’s Kingdom is yours.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed are you who hunger now,  for you will be filled.  Blessed are you who weep now,  for you will laugh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed are you when men shall hate you, and when they shall exclude and mock you, and throw out your name as evil, for the Son of Man’s sake.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Rejoice in that day, and leap for joy, for behold, your reward is great in heaven, for their fathers did the same thing to the prophets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But woe to you who are rich!  For you have received your consolation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Woe to you, you who are full now,  for you will be hungry.  Woe to you who laugh now,  for you will mourn and weep.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Woe,  when  men speak well of you,  for their fathers did the same thing to the false prophets.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But I tell you who hear: love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: bless those who curse you, and pray for those who mistreat you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: To him who strikes you on the cheek, offer also the other; and from him who takes away your cloak, don’t withhold your coat also.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Give to everyone who asks you, and don’t ask him who takes away your goods to give them back again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “As you would like people to do to you, do exactly so to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you lend to those from whom you hope to receive, what credit is that to you? Even sinners lend to sinners, to receive back as much.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But love your enemies, and do good, and lend, expecting nothing back; and your reward will be great, and you will be children of the Most High; for he is kind toward the unthankful and evil.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Therefore be merciful,  even as your Father is also merciful.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t judge,  and you won’t be judged.  Don’t condemn,  and you won’t be condemned.  Set free,  and you will be set free.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be given to you.  For with the same measure you measure it will be measured back to you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spoke a parable to them. “Can the blind guide the blind? Won’t they both fall into a pit?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A disciple is not above his teacher, but everyone when he is fully trained will be like his teacher.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why do you see the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye, but don’t consider the beam that is in your own eye?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or how can you tell your brother, ‘Brother, let me remove the speck of chaff that is in your eye,’ when you yourself don’t see the beam that is in your own eye? You hypocrite! First remove the beam from your own eye, and then you can see clearly to remove the speck of chaff that is in your brother’s eye.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For there is no good tree that produces rotten fruit; nor again a rotten tree that produces good fruit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For each tree is known by its own fruit. For people don’t gather figs from thorns, nor do they gather grapes from a bramble bush.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The good man out of the good treasure of his heart brings out that which is good, and the evil man out of the evil treasure of his heart brings out that which is evil, for out of the abundance of the heart, his mouth speaks.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Why do you call me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ and don’t do the things which I say?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Everyone who comes to me, and hears my words, and does them, I will show you who he is like.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He is like a man building a house, who dug and went deep, and laid a foundation on the rock. When a flood arose, the stream broke against that house, and could not shake it, because it was founded on the rock.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he who hears, and doesn’t do, is like a man who built a house on the earth without a foundation, against which the stream broke, and immediately it fell, and the ruin of that house was great.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: After he had finished speaking in the hearing of the people, he entered into Capernaum.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A certain centurion’s servant, who was dear to him, was sick and at the point of death.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he heard about Jesus, he sent to him elders of the Jews, asking him to come and save his servant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they came to Jesus, they begged him earnestly, saying, “He is worthy for you to do this for him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for he loves our nation, and he built our synagogue for us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus went with them. When he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying to him, “Lord, don’t trouble yourself, for I am not worthy for you to come under my roof.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I didn’t even think myself worthy to come to you; but say the word, and my servant will be healed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I also am a man placed under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go!’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus heard these things, he marveled at him, and turned and said to the multitude who followed him, “I tell you, I have not found such great faith, no, not in Israel.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who were sent, returning to the house, found that the servant who had been sick was well.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Soon afterwards, he went to a city called Nain. Many of his disciples, along with a great multitude, went with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when he came near to the gate of the city, behold, one who was dead was carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. Many people of the city were with her.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said to her, “Don’t cry.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He came near and touched the coffin, and the bearers stood still. He said, “Young man, I tell you, arise!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Fear took hold of all, and they glorified God, saying, “A great prophet has arisen among us!” and, “God has visited his people!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This report went out concerning him in the whole of Judea, and in all the surrounding region.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The disciples of John told him about all these things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: John, calling to himself two of his disciples, sent them to Jesus, saying, “Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for another?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the men had come to him, they said, “John the Baptizer has sent us to you, saying, ‘Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In that hour he cured many of diseases and plagues and evil spirits; and to many who were blind he gave sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you have seen and heard: that the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When John’s messengers had departed, he began to tell the multitudes about John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft clothing? Behold, those who are gorgeously dressed, and live delicately, are in kings’ courts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This is he of whom it is written,  ‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face,  who will prepare your way before you.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “For I tell you, among those who are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptizer, yet he who is least in God’s Kingdom is greater than he.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When all the people and the tax collectors heard this, they declared God to be just, having been baptized with John’s baptism.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the Pharisees and the lawyers rejected the counsel of God, not being baptized by him themselves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “To what then will I liken the people of this generation? What are they like?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They are like children who sit in the marketplace, and call to one another, saying, ‘We piped to you, and you didn’t dance. We mourned, and you didn’t weep.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For John the Baptizer came neither eating bread nor drinking wine, and you say, ‘He has a demon.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Son of Man has come eating and drinking, and you say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man, and a drunkard; a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Wisdom is justified by all her children.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One of the Pharisees invited him to eat with him. He entered into the Pharisee’s house, and sat at the table.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, a woman in the city who was a sinner, when she knew that he was reclining in the Pharisee’s house, she brought an alabaster jar of ointment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Standing behind at his feet weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears, and she wiped them with the hair of her head, kissed his feet, and anointed them with the ointment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now when the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he said to himself, “This man, if he were a prophet, would have perceived who and what kind of woman this is who touches him, that she is a sinner.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered him, “Simon, I have something to tell you.”  He said, “Teacher, say on.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “A certain lender had two debtors. The one owed five hundred denarii, and the other fifty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they couldn’t pay, he forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him most?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Simon answered, “He, I suppose, to whom he forgave the most.” He said to him, “You have judged correctly.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turning to the woman, he said to Simon, “Do you see this woman? I entered into your house, and you gave me no water for my feet, but she has wet my feet with her tears, and wiped them with the hair of her head.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You gave me no kiss, but she, since the time I came in, has not ceased to kiss my feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You didn’t anoint my head with oil, but she has anointed my feet with ointment.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore I tell you, her sins, which are many, are forgiven, for she loved much. But to whom little is forgiven, the same loves little.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to her, “Your sins are forgiven.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who sat at the table with him began to say to themselves, “Who is this who even forgives sins?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the woman, “Your faith has saved you. Go in peace.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Soon afterwards, he went about through cities and villages, preaching and bringing the good news of God’s Kingdom. With him were the twelve,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and certain women who had been healed of evil spirits and infirmities: Mary who was called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and Joanna, the wife of Chuzas, Herod’s steward; Susanna; and many others; who served them  from their possessions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When a great multitude came together, and people from every city were coming to him, he spoke by a parable.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The farmer went out to sow his seed. As he sowed, some fell along the road, and it was trampled under foot, and the birds of the sky devoured it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Other seed fell on the rock, and as soon as it grew, it withered away, because it had no moisture.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Other fell amid the thorns, and the thorns grew with it, and choked it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Other fell into the good ground, and grew, and produced one hundred times as much fruit.” As he said these things, he called out, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then his disciples asked him, “What does this parable mean?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “To you it is given to know the mysteries of God’s Kingdom, but to the rest in parables; that ‘seeing they may not see, and hearing they may not understand.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the parable is this: The seed is the word of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those along the road are those who hear, then the devil comes, and takes away the word from their heart, that they may not believe and be saved.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those on the rock are they who, when they hear, receive the word with joy; but these have no root, who believe for a while, then fall away in time of temptation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That which fell among the thorns, these are those who have heard, and as they go on their way they are choked with cares, riches, and pleasures of life, and bring no fruit to maturity.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That in the good ground, these are such as in an honest and good heart, having heard the word, hold it tightly, and produces fruit with patience.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “No one, when he has lit a lamp, covers it with a container, or puts it under a bed; but puts it on a stand, that those who enter in may see the light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For nothing is hidden, that will not be revealed; nor anything secret, that will not be known and come to light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be careful therefore how you hear. For whoever has, to him will be given; and whoever doesn’t have, from him will be taken away even that which he thinks he has.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: His mother and brothers came to him, and they could not come near him for the crowd.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Some people told him, “Your mother and your brothers stand outside, desiring to see you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he answered them, “My mother and my brothers are these who hear the word of God, and do it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now on one of those days, he entered into a boat, himself and his disciples, and he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side of the lake.” So they launched out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as they sailed, he fell asleep. A wind storm came down on the lake, and they were taking on dangerous amounts of water.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They came to him, and awoke him, saying, “Master, master, we are dying!” He awoke, and rebuked the wind and the raging of the water, and they ceased, and it was calm.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Where is your faith?” Being afraid they marveled, saying to one another, “Who is this, then, that he commands even the winds and the water, and they obey him?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They arrived at the country of the Gadarenes, which is opposite Galilee.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus stepped ashore, a certain man out of the city who had demons for a long time met him. He wore no clothes, and didn’t live in a house, but in the tombs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he saw Jesus, he cried out, and fell down before him, and with a loud voice said, “What do I have to do with you, Jesus, you Son of the Most High God? I beg you, don’t torment me!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For Jesus was commanding the unclean spirit to come out of the man. For the unclean spirit had often seized the man. He was kept under guard, and bound with chains and fetters. Breaking the bands apart, he was driven by the demon into the desert.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” He said, “Legion,” for many demons had entered into him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They begged him that he would not command them to go into the abyss.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now there was there a herd of many pigs feeding on the mountain, and they begged him that he would allow them to enter into those. He allowed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The demons came out of the man, and entered into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake, and were drowned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When those who fed them saw what had happened, they fled, and told it in the city and in the country.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: People went out to see what had happened. They came to Jesus, and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, sitting at Jesus’ feet, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who saw it told them how he who had been possessed by demons was healed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All the people of the surrounding country of the Gadarenes asked him to depart from them, for they were very much afraid. He entered into the boat, and returned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the man from whom the demons had gone out begged him that he might go with him, but Jesus sent him away, saying,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Return to your house, and declare what great things God has done for you.” He went his way, proclaiming throughout the whole city what great things Jesus had done for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus returned, the multitude welcomed him, for they were all waiting for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, there came a man named Jairus, and he was a ruler of the synagogue. He fell down at Jesus’ feet, and begged him to come into his house,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for he had an only daughter, about twelve years of age, and she was dying. But as he went, the multitudes pressed against him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years, who had spent all her living on physicians, and could not be healed by any,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: came behind him, and touched the fringe  of his cloak, and immediately the flow of her blood stopped.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said, “Who touched me?” When all denied it, Peter and those with him said, “Master, the multitudes press and jostle you, and you say, ‘Who touched me?’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said, “Someone did touch me, for I perceived that power has gone out of me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the woman saw that she was not hidden, she came trembling, and falling down before him declared to him in the presence of all the people the reason why she had touched him, and how she was healed immediately.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to her, “Daughter, cheer up. Your faith has made you well. Go in peace.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he still spoke, one from the ruler of the synagogue’s house came, saying to him, “Your daughter is dead. Don’t trouble the Teacher.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus hearing it, answered him, “Don’t be afraid. Only believe, and she will be healed.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he came to the house, he didn’t allow anyone to enter in, except Peter, John, James, the father of the child, and her mother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: All were weeping and mourning her, but he said, “Don’t weep. She isn’t dead, but sleeping.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were ridiculing him, knowing that she was dead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he put them all outside, and taking her by the hand, he called, saying, “Child, arise!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her spirit returned, and she rose up immediately. He commanded that something be given to her to eat.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Her parents were amazed, but he commanded them to tell no one what had been done.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called the twelve  together, and gave them power and authority over all demons, and to cure diseases.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sent them out to preach God’s Kingdom and to heal the sick.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Take nothing for your journey — neither staffs, nor wallet, nor bread, nor money; neither have two coats apiece.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Into whatever house you enter, stay there, and depart from there.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As many as don’t receive you, when you depart from that city, shake off even the dust from your feet for a testimony against them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They departed, and went throughout the villages, preaching the Good News, and healing everywhere.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by him; and he was very perplexed, because it was said by some that John had risen from the dead,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and by some that Elijah had appeared, and by others that one of the old prophets had risen again.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Herod said, “John I beheaded, but who is this, about whom I hear such things?” He sought to see him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The apostles, when they had returned, told him what things they had done. He took them, and withdrew apart to a deserted place of a city called Bethsaida.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the multitudes, perceiving it, followed him. He welcomed them, and spoke to them of God’s Kingdom, and he cured those who needed healing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The day began to wear away; and the twelve came, and said to him, “Send the multitude away, that they may go into the surrounding villages and farms, and lodge, and get food, for we are here in a deserted place.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said to them, “You give them something to eat.” They said, “We have no more than five loaves and two fish, unless we should go and buy food for all these people.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they were about five thousand men. He said to his disciples, “Make them sit down in groups of about fifty each.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They did so, and made them all sit down.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to the sky, he blessed them, and broke them, and gave them to the disciples to set before the multitude.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They ate, and were all filled. They gathered up twelve baskets of broken pieces that were left over.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he was praying alone, the disciples were with him, and he asked them, “Who do the multitudes say that I am?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They answered, “‘John the Baptizer,’ but others say, ‘Elijah,’ and others, that one of the old prophets is risen again.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered, “The Christ of God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he warned them, and commanded them to tell this to no one,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, “The Son of Man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to all, “If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross,  and follow me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever will lose his life for my sake, the same will save it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For what does it profit a man if he gains the whole world, and loses or forfeits his own self?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words, of him will the Son of Man be ashamed, when he comes in his glory, and the glory of the Father, and of the holy angels.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I tell you the truth: There are some of those who stand here, who will in no way taste of death, until they see God’s Kingdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: About eight days after these sayings, he took with him Peter, John, and James, and went up onto the mountain to pray.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he was praying, the appearance of his face was altered, and his clothing became white and dazzling.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, two men were talking with him, who were Moses and Elijah,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who appeared in glory, and spoke of his departure,  which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now Peter and those who were with him were heavy with sleep, but when they were fully awake, they saw his glory, and the two men who stood with him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they were parting from him, Peter said to Jesus, “Master, it is good for us to be here. Let’s make three tents: one for you, and one for Moses, and one for Elijah,” not knowing what he said.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he said these things, a cloud came and overshadowed them, and they were afraid as they entered into the cloud.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son. Listen to him!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the voice came, Jesus was found alone. They were silent, and told no one in those days any of the things which they had seen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the next day, when they had come down from the mountain, a great multitude met him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, a man from the crowd called out, saying, “Teacher, I beg you to look at my son, for he is my only child.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, a spirit takes him, he suddenly cries out, and it convulses him so that he foams, and it hardly departs from him, bruising him severely.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I begged your disciples to cast it out, and they couldn’t.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you and bear with you? Bring your son here.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: While he was still coming, the demon threw him down and convulsed him violently. But Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the boy, and gave him back to his father.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were all astonished at the majesty of God. But while all were marveling at all the things which Jesus did, he said to his disciples,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Let these words sink into your ears, for the Son of Man will be delivered up into the hands of men.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they didn’t understand this saying. It was concealed from them, that they should not perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this saying.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There arose an argument among them about which of them was the greatest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, perceiving the reasoning of their hearts, took a little child, and set him by his side,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and said to them, “Whoever receives this little child in my name receives me. Whoever receives me receives him who sent me. For whoever is least among you all, this one will be great.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: John answered, “Master, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we forbade him, because he doesn’t follow with us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “Don’t forbid him, for he who is not against us is for us.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It came to pass, when the days were near that he should be taken up, he intently set his face to go to Jerusalem,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and sent messengers before his face. They went, and entered into a village of the Samaritans, so as to prepare for him.

translations.name: World English Bible
