translations.text: They didn’t receive him, because he was traveling with his face set towards Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When his disciples, James and John, saw this, they said, “Lord, do you want us to command fire to come down from the sky, and destroy them, just as Elijah did?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he turned and rebuked them, “You don’t know of what kind of spirit you are.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Son of Man didn’t come to destroy men’s lives, but to save them.” They went to another village.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they went on the way, a certain man said to him, “I want to follow you wherever you go, Lord.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “The foxes have holes, and the birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to another, “Follow me!” But he said, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to him, “Leave the dead to bury their own dead, but you go and announce God’s Kingdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Another also said, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first allow me to say good-bye to those who are at my house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to him, “No one, having put his hand to the plow, and looking back, is fit for God’s Kingdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now after these things, the Lord also appointed seventy others, and sent them two by two ahead of him  into every city and place, where he was about to come.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said to them, “The harvest is indeed plentiful, but the laborers are few. Pray therefore to the Lord of the harvest, that he may send out laborers into his harvest.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Go your ways. Behold, I send you out as lambs among wolves.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Carry no purse, nor wallet, nor sandals. Greet no one on the way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Into whatever house you enter, first say, ‘Peace be to this house.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If a son of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; but if not, it will return to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remain in that same house, eating and drinking the things they give, for the laborer is worthy of his wages. Don’t go from house to house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Into whatever city you enter, and they receive you, eat the things that are set before you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Heal the sick who are therein, and tell them, ‘God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But into whatever city you enter, and they don’t receive you, go out into its streets and say,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘Even the dust from your city that clings to us, we wipe off against you. Nevertheless know this, that God’s Kingdom has come near to you.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you, it will be more tolerable in that day for Sodom than for that city.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in the judgment than for you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever listens to you listens to me, and whoever rejects you rejects me. Whoever rejects me rejects him who sent me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The seventy returned with joy, saying, “Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “I saw Satan having fallen like lightning from heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, I give you authority to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will in any way hurt you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless, don’t rejoice in this, that the spirits are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In that same hour Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit, and said, “I thank you, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turning to the disciples, he said, “All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows who the Son is, except the Father, and who the Father is, except the Son, and he to whomever the Son desires to reveal him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Turning to the disciples, he said privately, “Blessed are the eyes which see the things that you see,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see the things which you see, and didn’t see them, and to hear the things which you hear, and didn’t hear them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, a certain lawyer stood up and tested him, saying, “Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “What is written in the law? How do you read it?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your strength, and with all your mind;  and your neighbor as yourself.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, “You have answered correctly. Do this, and you will live.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he, desiring to justify himself, asked Jesus, “Who is my neighbor?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, “A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and he fell among robbers, who both stripped him and beat him, and departed, leaving him half dead.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: By chance a certain priest was going down that way. When he saw him, he passed by on the other side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In the same way a Levite also, when he came to the place, and saw him, passed by on the other side.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But a certain Samaritan, as he traveled, came where he was. When he saw him, he was moved with compassion,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: came to him, and bound up his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. He set him on his own animal, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On the next day, when he departed, he took out two denarii, and gave them to the host, and said to him, ‘Take care of him. Whatever you spend beyond that, I will repay you when I return.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now which of these three do you think seemed to be a neighbor to him who fell among the robbers?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “He who showed mercy on him.” Then Jesus said to him, “Go and do likewise.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they went on their way, he entered into a certain village, and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: She had a sister called Mary, who also sat at Jesus’ feet, and heard his word.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But Martha was distracted with much serving, and she came up to him, and said, “Lord, don’t you care that my sister left me to serve alone? Ask her therefore to help me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the good part, which will not be taken away from her.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he finished praying in a certain place, one of his disciples said to him, “Lord, teach us to pray, just as John also taught his disciples.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “When you pray, say,  ‘Our Father in heaven,  may your name be kept holy.  May your Kingdom come.  May your will be done on earth, as it is in heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Give us day by day our daily bread.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Forgive us our sins,  for we ourselves also forgive everyone who is indebted to us.   Bring us not into temptation,  but deliver us from the evil one.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Which of you, if you go to a friend at midnight, and tell him, ‘Friend, lend me three loaves of bread,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for a friend of mine has come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him,’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he from within will answer and say, ‘Don’t bother me. The door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed. I can’t get up and give it to you’?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you, although he will not rise and give it to him because he is his friend, yet because of his persistence, he will get up and give him as many as he needs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I tell you, keep asking, and it will be given you. Keep seeking, and you will find. Keep knocking, and it will be opened to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For everyone who asks receives. He who seeks finds. To him who knocks it will be opened.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Which of you fathers, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, he won’t give him a snake instead of a fish, will he?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or if he asks for an egg, he won’t give him a scorpion, will he?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask him?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was casting out a demon, and it was mute. When the demon had gone out, the mute man spoke; and the multitudes marveled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But some of them said, “He casts out demons by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Others, testing him, sought from him a sign from heaven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he, knowing their thoughts, said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation. A house divided against itself falls.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If Satan also is divided against himself, how will his kingdom stand? For you say that I cast out demons by Beelzebul.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore will they be your judges.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if I by God’s finger cast out demons, then God’s Kingdom has come to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “When the strong man, fully armed, guards his own dwelling, his goods are safe.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when someone stronger attacks him and overcomes him, he takes from him his whole armor in which he trusted, and divides his plunder.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He that is not with me is against me. He who doesn’t gather with me scatters.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The unclean spirit, when he has gone out of the man, passes through dry places, seeking rest, and finding none, he says, ‘I will turn back to my house from which I came out.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he returns, he finds it swept and put in order.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he goes, and takes seven other spirits more evil than himself, and they enter in and dwell there. The last state of that man becomes worse than the first.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It came to pass, as he said these things, a certain woman out of the multitude lifted up her voice, and said to him, “Blessed is the womb that bore you, and the breasts which nursed you!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said, “On the contrary, blessed are those who hear the word of God, and keep it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the multitudes were gathering together to him, he began to say, “This is an evil generation. It seeks after a sign. No sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah, the prophet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For even as Jonah became a sign to the Ninevites, so will also the Son of Man be to this generation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and will condemn them: for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, one greater than Solomon is here.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation, and will condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and behold, one greater than Jonah is here.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “No one, when he has lit a lamp, puts it in a cellar or under a basket, but on a stand, that those who come in may see the light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lamp of the body is the eye. Therefore when your eye is good, your whole body is also full of light; but when it is evil, your body also is full of darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore see whether the light that is in you isn’t darkness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If therefore your whole body is full of light, having no part dark, it will be wholly full of light, as when the lamp with its bright shining gives you light.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now as he spoke, a certain Pharisee asked him to dine with him. He went in, and sat at the table.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When the Pharisee saw it, he marveled that he had not first washed himself before dinner.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Lord said to him, “Now you Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and of the platter, but your inward part is full of extortion and wickedness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You foolish ones, didn’t he who made the outside make the inside also?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But give for gifts to the needy those things which are within, and behold, all things will be clean to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But woe to you Pharisees! For you tithe mint and rue and every herb, but you bypass justice and God’s love. You ought to have done these, and not to have left the other undone.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Woe to you Pharisees! For you love the best seats in the synagogues, and the greetings in the marketplaces.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like hidden graves, and the men who walk over them don’t know it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One of the lawyers answered him, “Teacher, in saying this you insult us also.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “Woe to you lawyers also! For you load men with burdens that are difficult to carry, and you yourselves won’t even lift one finger to help carry those burdens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Woe to you! For you build the tombs of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So you testify and consent to the works of your fathers. For they killed them, and you build their tombs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore also the wisdom of God said, ‘I will send to them prophets and apostles; and some of them they will kill and persecute,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zachariah, who perished between the altar and the sanctuary.’ Yes, I tell you, it will be required of this generation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Woe to you lawyers! For you took away the key of knowledge. You didn’t enter in yourselves, and those who were entering in, you hindered.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he said these things to them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to be terribly angry, and to draw many things out of him;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: lying in wait for him, and seeking to catch him in something he might say, that they might accuse him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Meanwhile, when a multitude of many thousands had gathered together, so much so that they trampled on each other, he began to tell his disciples first of all, “Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But there is nothing covered up, that will not be revealed, nor hidden, that will not be known.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore whatever you have said in the darkness will be heard in the light. What you have spoken in the ear in the inner rooms will be proclaimed on the housetops.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I tell you, my friends, don’t be afraid of those who kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I will warn you whom you should fear. Fear him, who after he has killed, has power to cast into Gehenna.  Yes, I tell you, fear him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Aren’t five sparrows sold for two assaria coins  ? Not one of them is forgotten by God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the very hairs of your head are all counted. Therefore don’t be afraid. You are of more value than many sparrows.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I tell you, everyone who confesses me before men, him will the Son of Man also confess before the angels of God;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but he who denies me in the presence of men will be denied in the presence of the angels of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Everyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but those who blaspheme against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they bring you before the synagogues, the rulers, and the authorities, don’t be anxious how or what you will answer, or what you will say;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for the Holy Spirit will teach you in that same hour what you must say.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One of the multitude said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said to him, “Man, who made me a judge or an arbitrator over you?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Beware! Keep yourselves from covetousness, for a man’s life doesn’t consist of the abundance of the things which he possesses.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spoke a parable to them, saying, “The ground of a certain rich man produced abundantly.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He reasoned within himself, saying, ‘What will I do, because I don’t have room to store my crops?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, ‘This is what I will do. I will pull down my barns, and build bigger ones, and there I will store all my grain and my goods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will tell my soul, “Soul, you have many goods laid up for many years. Take your ease, eat, drink, be merry.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But God said to him, ‘You foolish one, tonight your soul is required of you. The things which you have prepared — whose will they be?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So is he who lays up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to his disciples, “Therefore I tell you, don’t be anxious for your life, what you will eat, nor yet for your body, what you will wear.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Life is more than food, and the body is more than clothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Consider the ravens: they don’t sow, they don’t reap, they have no warehouse or barn, and God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than birds!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Which of you by being anxious can add a cubit  to his height?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If then you aren’t able to do even the least things, why are you anxious about the rest?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Consider the lilies, how they grow. They don’t toil, neither do they spin; yet I tell you, even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if this is how God clothes the grass in the field, which today exists, and tomorrow is cast into the oven, how much more will he clothe you, O you of little faith?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t seek what you will eat or what you will drink; neither be anxious.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the nations of the world seek after all of these things, but your Father knows that you need these things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But seek God’s Kingdom, and all these things will be added to you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be afraid, little flock, for it is your Father’s good pleasure to give you the Kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Sell that which you have, and give gifts to the needy. Make for yourselves purses which don’t grow old, a treasure in the heavens that doesn’t fail, where no thief approaches, neither moth destroys.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Let your waist be dressed and your lamps burning.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be like men watching for their lord, when he returns from the marriage feast; that, when he comes and knocks, they may immediately open to him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed are those servants, whom the lord will find watching when he comes. Most certainly I tell you, that he will dress himself, and make them recline, and will come and serve them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will be blessed if he comes in the second or third watch, and finds them so.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what hour the thief was coming, he would have watched, and not allowed his house to be broken into.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore be ready also, for the Son of Man is coming in an hour that you don’t expect him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Peter said to him, “Lord, are you telling this parable to us, or to everybody?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Lord said, “Who then is the faithful and wise steward, whom his lord will set over his household, to give them their portion of food at the right times?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Blessed is that servant whom his lord will find doing so when he comes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Truly I tell you, that he will set him over all that he has.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if that servant says in his heart, ‘My lord delays his coming,’ and begins to beat the menservants and the maidservants, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: then the lord of that servant will come in a day when he isn’t expecting him, and in an hour that he doesn’t know, and will cut him in two, and place his portion with the unfaithful.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That servant, who knew his lord’s will, and didn’t prepare, nor do what he wanted, will be beaten with many stripes,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but he who didn’t know, and did things worthy of stripes, will be beaten with few stripes. To whomever much is given, of him will much be required; and to whom much was entrusted, of him more will be asked.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “I came to throw fire on the earth. I wish it were already kindled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how distressed I am until it is accomplished!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Do you think that I have come to give peace in the earth? I tell you, no, but rather division.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For from now on, there will be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will be divided, father against son, and son against father; mother against daughter, and daughter against her mother; mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law, and daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the multitudes also, “When you see a cloud rising from the west, immediately you say, ‘A shower is coming,’ and so it happens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When a south wind blows, you say, ‘There will be a scorching heat,’ and it happens.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but how is it that you don’t interpret this time?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For when you are going with your adversary before the magistrate, try diligently on the way to be released from him, lest perhaps he drag you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the officer, and the officer throw you into prison.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you, you will by no means get out of there, until you have paid the very last penny. ”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now there were some present at the same time who told him about the Galileans, whose blood Pilate had mixed with their sacrifices.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered them, “Do you think that these Galileans were worse sinners than all the other Galileans, because they suffered such things?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you, no, but unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or those eighteen, on whom the tower in Siloam fell, and killed them; do you think that they were worse offenders than all the men who dwell in Jerusalem?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you, no, but, unless you repent, you will all perish in the same way.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spoke this parable. “A certain man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard, and he came seeking fruit on it, and found none.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the vine dresser, ‘Behold, these three years I have come looking for fruit on this fig tree, and found none. Cut it down. Why does it waste the soil?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered, ‘Lord, leave it alone this year also, until I dig around it, and fertilize it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If it bears fruit, fine; but if not, after that, you can cut it down.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was teaching in one of the synagogues on the Sabbath day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, there was a woman who had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and she was bent over, and could in no way straighten herself up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus saw her, he called her, and said to her, “Woman, you are freed from your infirmity.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He laid his hands on her, and immediately she stood up straight, and glorified God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The ruler of the synagogue, being indignant because Jesus had healed on the Sabbath, said to the multitude, “There are six days in which men ought to work. Therefore come on those days and be healed, and not on the Sabbath day!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore the Lord answered him, “You hypocrites! Doesn’t each one of you free his ox or his donkey from the stall on the Sabbath, and lead him away to water?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan had bound eighteen long years, be freed from this bondage on the Sabbath day?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he said these things, all his adversaries were disappointed, and all the multitude rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “What is God’s Kingdom like? To what shall I compare it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and put in his own garden. It grew, and became a large tree, and the birds of the sky live in its branches.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Again he said, “To what shall I compare God’s Kingdom?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is like yeast, which a woman took and hid in three measures   of flour, until it was all leavened.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went on his way through cities and villages, teaching, and traveling on to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One said to him, “Lord, are they few who are saved?” He said to them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Strive to enter in by the narrow door, for many, I tell you, will seek to enter in, and will not be able.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When once the master of the house has risen up, and has shut the door, and you begin to stand outside, and to knock at the door, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us!’ then he will answer and tell you, ‘I don’t know you or where you come from.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then you will begin to say, ‘We ate and drank in your presence, and you taught in our streets.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will say, ‘I tell you, I don’t know where you come from. Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when you see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets, in God’s Kingdom, and yourselves being thrown outside.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will come from the east, west, north, and south, and will sit down in God’s Kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, there are some who are last who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On that same day, some Pharisees came, saying to him, “Get out of here, and go away, for Herod wants to kill you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Go and tell that fox, ‘Behold, I cast out demons and perform cures today and tomorrow, and the third day I complete my mission.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Nevertheless I must go on my way today and tomorrow and the next day, for it can’t be that a prophet perish outside of Jerusalem.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, that kills the prophets, and stones those who are sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, like a hen gathers her own brood under her wings, and you refused!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, your house is left to you desolate. I tell you, you will not see me, until you say, ‘Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he went into the house of one of the rulers of the Pharisees on a Sabbath to eat bread, they were watching him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, a certain man who had dropsy was in front of him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, answering, spoke to the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But they were silent. He took him, and healed him, and let him go.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered them, “Which of you, if your son  or an ox fell into a well, wouldn’t immediately pull him out on a Sabbath day?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They couldn’t answer him regarding these things.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spoke a parable to those who were invited, when he noticed how they chose the best seats, and said to them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “When you are invited by anyone to a marriage feast, don’t sit in the best seat, since perhaps someone more honorable than you might be invited by him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and he who invited both of you would come and tell you, ‘Make room for this person.’ Then you would begin, with shame, to take the lowest place.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when you are invited, go and sit in the lowest place, so that when he who invited you comes, he may tell you, ‘Friend, move up higher.’ Then you will be honored in the presence of all who sit at the table with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also said to the one who had invited him, “When you make a dinner or a supper, don’t call your friends, nor your brothers, nor your kinsmen, nor rich neighbors, or perhaps they might also return the favor, and pay you back.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when you make a feast, ask the poor, the maimed, the lame, or the blind;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and you will be blessed, because they don’t have the resources to repay you. For you will be repaid in the resurrection of the righteous.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When one of those who sat at the table with him heard these things, he said to him, “Blessed is he who will feast in God’s Kingdom!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said to him, “A certain man made a great supper, and he invited many people.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sent out his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited, ‘Come, for everything is ready now.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They all as one began to make excuses.  “The first said to him, ‘I have bought a field, and I must go and see it. Please have me excused.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Another said, ‘I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I must go try them out. Please have me excused.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Another said, ‘I have married a wife, and therefore I can’t come.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “That servant came, and told his lord these things. Then the master of the house, being angry, said to his servant, ‘Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in the poor, maimed, blind, and lame.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The servant said, ‘Lord, it is done as you commanded, and there is still room.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The lord said to the servant, ‘Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I tell you that none of those men who were invited will taste of my supper.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now great multitudes were going with him. He turned and said to them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “If anyone comes to me, and doesn’t disregard  his own father, mother, wife, children, brothers, and sisters, yes, and his own life also, he can’t be my disciple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever doesn’t bear his own cross, and come after me, can’t be my disciple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For which of you, desiring to build a tower, doesn’t first sit down and count the cost, to see if he has enough to complete it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or perhaps, when he has laid a foundation, and is not able to finish, everyone who sees begins to mock him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, ‘This man began to build, and wasn’t able to finish.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or what king, as he goes to encounter another king in war, will not sit down first and consider whether he is able with ten thousand to meet him who comes against him with twenty thousand?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sends an envoy, and asks for conditions of peace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So therefore whoever of you who doesn’t renounce all that he has, he can’t be my disciple.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Salt is good, but if the salt becomes flat and tasteless, with what do you season it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It is fit neither for the soil nor for the manure pile. It is thrown out. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming close to him to hear him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees and the scribes murmured, saying, “This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He told them this parable.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Which of you men, if you had one hundred sheep, and lost one of them, wouldn’t leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness, and go after the one that was lost, until he found it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he has found it, he carries it on his shoulders, rejoicing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you that even so there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety-nine righteous people who need no repentance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Or what woman, if she had ten drachma  coins, if she lost one drachma coin, wouldn’t light a lamp, sweep the house, and seek diligently until she found it?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the drachma which I had lost.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner repenting.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “A certain man had two sons.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me my share of your property.’ He divided his livelihood between them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Not many days after, the younger son gathered all of this together and traveled into a far country. There he wasted his property with riotous living.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he had spent all of it, there arose a severe famine in that country, and he began to be in need.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He went and joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He wanted to fill his belly with the husks that the pigs ate, but no one gave him any.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when he came to himself he said, ‘How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough to spare, and I’m dying with hunger!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I will get up and go to my father, and will tell him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am no more worthy to be called your son. Make me as one of your hired servants.”’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He arose, and came to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him, and was moved with compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring out the best robe, and put it on him. Put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let us eat, and celebrate;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for this, my son, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’ They began to celebrate.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now his elder son was in the field. As he came near to the house, he heard music and dancing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called one of the servants to him, and asked what was going on.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and healthy.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he was angry, and would not go in. Therefore his father came out, and begged him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he answered his father, ‘Behold, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed a commandment of yours, but you never gave me a goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when this, your son, came, who has devoured your living with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it was appropriate to celebrate and be glad, for this, your brother, was dead, and is alive again. He was lost, and is found.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also said to his disciples, “There was a certain rich man who had a manager. An accusation was made to him that this man was wasting his possessions.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called him, and said to him, ‘What is this that I hear about you? Give an accounting of your management, for you can no longer be manager.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The manager said within himself, ‘What will I do, seeing that my lord is taking away the management position from me? I don’t have strength to dig. I am ashamed to beg.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I know what I will do, so that when I am removed from management, they may receive me into their houses.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Calling each one of his lord’s debtors to him, he said to the first, ‘How much do you owe to my lord?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, ‘A hundred batos  of oil.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and sit down quickly and write fifty.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said to another, ‘How much do you owe?’ He said, ‘A hundred cors  of wheat.’ He said to him, ‘Take your bill, and write eighty.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “His lord commended the dishonest manager because he had done wisely, for the children of this world are, in their own generation, wiser than the children of the light.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you, make for yourselves friends by means of unrighteous mammon, so that when you fail, they may receive you into the eternal tents.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If therefore you have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you have not been faithful in that which is another’s, who will give you that which is your own?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: No servant can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to one, and despise the other. You aren’t able to serve God and Mammon.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Pharisees, who were lovers of money, also heard all these things, and they scoffed at him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “You are those who justify yourselves in the sight of men, but God knows your hearts. For that which is exalted among men is an abomination in the sight of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The law and the prophets were until John. From that time the Good News of God’s Kingdom is preached, and everyone is forcing his way into it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away, than for one tiny stroke of a pen in the law to fall.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Everyone who divorces his wife, and marries another, commits adultery. He who marries one who is divorced from a husband commits adultery.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Now there was a certain rich man, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, living in luxury every day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A certain beggar, named Lazarus, was laid at his gate, full of sores,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and desiring to be fed with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s table. Yes, even the dogs came and licked his sores.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The beggar died, and he was carried away by the angels to Abraham’s bosom. The rich man also died, and was buried.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In Hades,  he lifted up his eyes, being in torment, and saw Abraham far off, and Lazarus at his bosom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He cried and said, ‘Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue! For I am in anguish in this flame.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But Abraham said, ‘Son, remember that you, in your lifetime, received your good things, and Lazarus, in the same way, bad things. But now here he is comforted and you are in anguish.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Besides all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed, that those who want to pass from here to you are not able, and that no one may cross over from there to us.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He said, ‘I ask you therefore, father, that you would send him to my father’s house;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for I have five brothers, that he may testify to them, so they won’t also come into this place of torment.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But Abraham said to him, ‘They have Moses and the prophets. Let them listen to them.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He said, ‘No, father Abraham, but if one goes to them from the dead, they will repent.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He said to him, ‘If they don’t listen to Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded if one rises from the dead.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the disciples, “It is impossible that no occasions of stumbling should come, but woe to him through whom they come!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea, rather than that he should cause one of these little ones to stumble.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Be careful. If your brother sins against you, rebuke him. If he repents, forgive him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If he sins against you seven times in the day, and seven times returns, saying, ‘I repent,’ you shall forgive him.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The apostles said to the Lord, “Increase our faith.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Lord said, “If you had faith like a grain of mustard seed, you would tell this sycamore tree, ‘Be uprooted, and be planted in the sea,’ and it would obey you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But who is there among you, having a servant plowing or keeping sheep, that will say, when he comes in from the field, ‘Come immediately and sit down at the table,’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and will not rather tell him, ‘Prepare my supper, clothe yourself properly, and serve me, while I eat and drink. Afterward you shall eat and drink’?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Does he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded? I think not.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even so you also, when you have done all the things that are commanded you, say, ‘We are unworthy servants. We have done our duty.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he was on his way to Jerusalem, he was passing along the borders of Samaria and Galilee.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he entered into a certain village, ten men who were lepers met him, who stood at a distance.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They lifted up their voices, saying, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he saw them, he said to them, “Go and show yourselves to the priests.” As they went, they were cleansed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: One of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, glorifying God with a loud voice.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He fell on his face at Jesus’ feet, giving him thanks; and he was a Samaritan.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, “Weren’t the ten cleansed? But where are the nine?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Were there none found who returned to give glory to God, except this stranger?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then he said to him, “Get up, and go your way. Your faith has healed you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Being asked by the Pharisees when God’s Kingdom would come, he answered them, “God’s Kingdom doesn’t come with observation;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: neither will they say, ‘Look, here!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ for behold, God’s Kingdom is within you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to the disciples, “The days will come, when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will tell you, ‘Look, here!’ or ‘Look, there!’ Don’t go away, nor follow after them,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for as the lightning, when it flashes out of the one part under the sky, shines to the other part under the sky; so will the Son of Man be in his day.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But first, he must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As it was in the days of Noah, even so will it be also in the days of the Son of Man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They ate, they drank, they married, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ship, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Likewise, even as it was in the days of Lot: they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but in the day that Lot went out from Sodom, it rained fire and sulfur from the sky, and destroyed them all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: It will be the same way in the day that the Son of Man is revealed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: In that day, he who will be on the housetop, and his goods in the house, let him not go down to take them away. Let him who is in the field likewise not turn back.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Remember Lot’s wife!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Whoever seeks to save his life loses it, but whoever loses his life preserves it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you, in that night there will be two people in one bed. The one will be taken, and the other will be left.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There will be two grinding grain together. One will be taken, and the other will be left.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They, answering, asked him, “Where, Lord?” He said to them, “Where the body is, there will the vultures also be gathered together.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He also spoke a parable to them that they must always pray, and not give up,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, “There was a judge in a certain city who didn’t fear God, and didn’t respect man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A widow was in that city, and she often came to him, saying, ‘Defend me from my adversary!’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He wouldn’t for a while, but afterward he said to himself, ‘Though I neither fear God, nor respect man,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yet because this widow bothers me, I will defend her, or else she will wear me out by her continual coming.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Lord said, “Listen to what the unrighteous judge says.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Won’t God avenge his chosen ones, who are crying out to him day and night, and yet he exercises patience with them?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you that he will avenge them quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will he find faith on the earth?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He spoke also this parable to certain people who were convinced of their own righteousness, and who despised all others.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “Two men went up into the temple to pray; one was a Pharisee, and the other was a tax collector.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Pharisee stood and prayed to himself like this: ‘God, I thank you, that I am not like the rest of men, extortionists, unrighteous, adulterers, or even like this tax collector.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I fast twice a week. I give tithes of all that I get.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the tax collector, standing far away, wouldn’t even lift up his eyes to heaven, but beat his breast, saying, ‘God, be merciful to me, a sinner!’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other; for everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, but he who humbles himself will be exalted.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They were also bringing their babies to him, that he might touch them. But when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus summoned them, saying, “Allow the little children to come to me, and don’t hinder them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Most certainly, I tell you, whoever doesn’t receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A certain ruler asked him, saying, “Good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus asked him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good, except one — God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You know the commandments: ‘Don’t commit adultery,’ ‘Don’t murder,’ ‘Don’t steal,’ ‘Don’t give false testimony,’ ‘Honor your father and your mother.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said, “I have observed all these things from my youth up.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus heard these things, he said to him, “You still lack one thing. Sell all that you have, and distribute it to the poor. You will have treasure in heaven. Come, follow me.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when he heard these things, he became very sad, for he was very rich.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus, seeing that he became very sad, said, “How hard it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For it is easier for a camel to enter in through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who heard it said, “Then who can be saved?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he said, “The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Peter said, “Look, we have left everything, and followed you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or wife, or brothers, or parents, or children, for God’s Kingdom’s sake,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who will not receive many times more in this time, and in the world to come, eternal life.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He took the twelve aside, and said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all the things that are written through the prophets concerning the Son of Man will be completed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he will be delivered up to the Gentiles, will be mocked, treated shamefully, and spit on.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They will scourge and kill him. On the third day, he will rise again.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They understood none of these things. This saying was hidden from them, and they didn’t understand the things that were said.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he came near Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the road, begging.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Hearing a multitude going by, he asked what this meant.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They told him that Jesus of Nazareth was passing by.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He cried out, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who led the way rebuked him, that he should be quiet; but he cried out all the more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Standing still, Jesus commanded him to be brought to him. When he had come near, he asked him,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “What do you want me to do?” He said, “Lord, that I may see again.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God. All the people, when they saw it, praised God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He entered and was passing through Jericho.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There was a man named Zacchaeus. He was a chief tax collector, and he was rich.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was trying to see who Jesus was, and couldn’t because of the crowd, because he was short.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He ran on ahead, and climbed up into a sycamore tree to see him, for he was going to pass that way.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When Jesus came to the place, he looked up and saw him, and said to him, “Zacchaeus, hurry and come down, for today I must stay at your house.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He hurried, came down, and received him joyfully.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When they saw it, they all murmured, saying, “He has gone in to lodge with a man who is a sinner.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord, “Behold, Lord, half of my goods I give to the poor. If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone, I restore four times as much.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, “Today, salvation has come to this house, because he also is a son of Abraham.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save that which was lost.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they heard these things, he went on and told a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and they supposed that God’s Kingdom would be revealed immediately.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said therefore, “A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He called ten servants of his, and gave them ten mina coins,   and told them, ‘Conduct business until I come.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But his citizens hated him, and sent an envoy after him, saying, ‘We don’t want this man to reign over us.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “When he had come back again, having received the kingdom, he commanded these servants, to whom he had given the money, to be called to him, that he might know what they had gained by conducting business.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The first came before him, saying, ‘Lord, your mina has made ten more minas.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He said to him, ‘Well done, you good servant! Because you were found faithful with very little, you shall have authority over ten cities.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “The second came, saying, ‘Your mina, Lord, has made five minas.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “So he said to him, ‘And you are to be over five cities.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Another came, saying, ‘Lord, behold, your mina, which I kept laid away in a handkerchief,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: for I feared you, because you are an exacting man. You take up that which you didn’t lay down, and reap that which you didn’t sow.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “He said to him, ‘Out of your own mouth will I judge you, you wicked servant! You knew that I am an exacting man, taking up that which I didn’t lay down, and reaping that which I didn’t sow.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then why didn’t you deposit my money in the bank, and at my coming, I might have earned interest on it?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to those who stood by, ‘Take the mina away from him, and give it to him who has the ten minas.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “They said to him, ‘Lord, he has ten minas!’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: ‘For I tell you that to everyone who has, will more be given; but from him who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But bring those enemies of mine who didn’t want me to reign over them here, and kill them before me.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Having said these things, he went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he came near to Bethsphage  and Bethany, at the mountain that is called Olivet, he sent two of his disciples,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, “Go your way into the village on the other side, in which, as you enter, you will find a colt tied, whereon no man ever yet sat. Untie it, and bring it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you untying it?’ say to him: ‘The Lord needs it.’”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who were sent went away, and found things just as he had told them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, “Why are you untying the colt?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They said, “The Lord needs it.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They brought it to Jesus. They threw their cloaks on the colt, and set Jesus on them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he went, they spread their cloaks on the road.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As he was now getting near, at the descent of the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works which they had seen,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, “Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord!  Peace in heaven, and glory in the highest!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Some of the Pharisees from the multitude said to him, “Teacher, rebuke your disciples!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered them, “I tell you that if these were silent, the stones would cry out.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: When he came near, he saw the city and wept over it,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying, “If you, even you, had known today the things which belong to your peace! But now, they are hidden from your eyes.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the days will come on you, when your enemies will throw up a barricade against you, surround you, hem you in on every side,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and will dash you and your children within you to the ground. They will not leave in you one stone on another, because you didn’t know the time of your visitation.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He entered into the temple, and began to drive out those who bought and sold in it,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: saying to them, “It is written, ‘My house is a house of prayer,’   but you have made it a ‘den of robbers’!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He was teaching daily in the temple, but the chief priests and the scribes and the leading men among the people sought to destroy him.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They couldn’t find what they might do, for all the people hung on to every word that he said.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: On one of those days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Good News, the priests and scribes came to him with the elders.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They asked him, “Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He answered them, “I also will ask you one question. Tell me:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They answered that they didn’t know where it was from.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He began to tell the people this parable. “A   man planted a vineyard, and rented it out to some farmers, and went into another country for a long time.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: At the proper season, he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him, and sent him away empty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sent yet another servant, and they also beat him, and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He sent yet a third, and they also wounded him, and threw him out.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The lord of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son. It may be that seeing him, they will respect him.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “But when the farmers saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He will come and destroy these farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.” When they heard it, they said, “May it never be!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he looked at them, and said, “Then what is this that is written,  ‘The stone which the builders rejected,  the same was made the chief cornerstone?’

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces,   but it will crush whomever it falls on to dust.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him that very hour, but they feared the people — for they knew he had spoken this parable against them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They watched him, and sent out spies, who pretended to be righteous, that they might trap him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They asked him, “Teacher, we know that you say and teach what is right, and aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them, “Why do you test me?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?”  They answered, “Caesar’s.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He said to them, “Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They weren’t able to trap him in his words before the people. They marveled at his answer, and were silent.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Some of the Sadducees came to him, those who deny that there is a resurrection.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They asked him, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife, and raise up children for his brother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There were therefore seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died childless.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The second took her as wife, and he died childless.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The third took her, and likewise the seven all left no children, and died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Afterward the woman also died.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be? For the seven had her as a wife.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to them, “The children of this age marry, and are given in marriage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For they can’t die any more, for they are like the angels, and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed at the bush, when he called the Lord ‘The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’

translations.name: World English Bible
